reasoning: I have voted CPI(M) since 1977—I was young then, I am old now, but this does not change. Yes, I know they will not win, I know nothing will change for me, my knees will still hurt and my grandson's roof will still leak. But I am 65 years old. I will not throw away my whole life and become something else for these two new parties fighting over our villages. My son can vote for who he wants. I vote for who I have always voted for. The Left is weak, but at least they were here when I was young, when the booth committee meant something real.
primary_drivers: Bone-deep political loyalty since 1977—cannot abandon at age 65
Distrust of BJP as outsiders and newcomers to this area
Cynicism that nothing changes anyway, so principle matters more than outcome
